title: HIPAA 164.308(a)(4)(ii)(B) — Cisco ISE Privileged Access Modification Audit
evidence: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence` with HIPAA tagging.

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA 164.308(a)(4)(ii)(B) — access establishment and modification. UC-17.1.71 produces continuous evidence of ISE admin role changes — the role that controls who can grant access to PHI-bearing networks.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrate that all changes to ISE admin privileges (which control access to PHI-bearing network systems) are logged with attribution.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.57' archiving admin role events to `audit_evidence` with `hipaa_clause=164.308(a)(4)(ii)(B)`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Grant a new admin super-admin. Evidence captured.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60050, 60051, 60052, 60100) earliest=-1d
| eval hipaa_clause="164.308(a)(4)(ii)(B)", control="Access_Establishment_Modification"
| table _time, admin_user, admin_role, admin_action, source_ip, hipaa_clause
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We continuously evidence HIPAA admin privilege modification on PHI-impacting NAC infrastructure.
value: HIPAA auditors require evidence of access modification. ISE admin role changes directly affect who can authorize access to PHI-bearing networks; this UC produces continuous evidence with attribution.
visualization: HIPAA dashboard: ISE admin role timeline.

references.title: UC-17.1.71 — Cisco ISE Admin Role Audit

knownFalsePositives: 1. Bulk role updates during planned admin reorg.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.71 operational.
- `audit_evidence` with 6y retention.

## Step 1-5

```ini
[UC-22.10.57: HIPAA — ISE Admin Role Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.10.57,reg=HIPAA,clause=164.308(a)(4)(ii)(B)
```
grandmaExplanation: We continuously evidence HIPAA admin privilege modification on PHI-impacting NAC infrastructure. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
